author: @anibalcyu3690
text:
Question:
The chemical from Minute 13:48 (Copper Oxide plus Formic Acid), once printed on a substrate, is sprayed or added to the component from Minute 14:08, which may be Ascorbic Acid, so that it reacts and leaves a thin layer of copper, or it's another reducing chemical.

Ascorbic Acid is a reducer and an antioxidant in the synthesis of copper nanoparticles.

Excuse my English.

author: @dondywondy
text:
I do agree with 10sheds21 below; please take care of yourself as your loss would be a loss to the actual world! Your knowledge and your inspiring videos must keep coming!

The first time I saw one of your videos, about a solar cooker, I commented about what I considered to be a mistake on your part. To my surprise you responded soon after and we had a short discussion. At the end I still felt you had erred. But, after watching so many of your videos, and being so impressed by your work style, I find I must apologize for first thinking you were being opinionated - I now see that you are a person of high intelligence and I do see YOUR side of the issue.

I know it takes a LOT of work to research, plan, record , edit and post your videos. Thank you! Please keep 'em coming!

text: I just wonder if the churning of cream to make butter might be a chemicomechanal process . author: @bartronicsecurity
text: Is it possible to coat plastic with that so as to have a base for electroplating stainless steel for final finish on the plastic part?

replies.author: @AnonymousAnarchist2
replies.text:
What plastic?

The answer varies, and frankly with the plastics Ive sent to plate they where never very chemically resistant so we used pure mechanical embedding.
Tollens reagent is also used to precoat plastics, just with a thin layer of silver, in a basic carrier rather then an acidic carrier.

But like Ive already said. I havent, I do not have the answers; but I do know you need to know your plastics properties and if they will react with the carrier and chemicals used.

replies.author: @bartronicsecurity
replies.text: @AnonymousAnarchist2Thanks.  Seems like I got lots learning to do in order to get my parts plated.

replies.author: @AnonymousAnarchist2
replies.text: @bartronicsecurity Well ua can just send them out, or for small volumes order a spary from 3M, but they will need to know the plastic they are made of.

author: @chrissscottt
text: Here's a poor-man's copper plate on steel method I discovered by accident. (well known by others too apparently) Put some sulphuric battery acid on a rag, rub an oxidized piece of copper pipe then with the same rag rub a piece of steel. Voila, a very thin but well bonded copper plate. Very pretty.
